name: CenterPoint Securities
description_short: Direct market access broker built for active day traders, offering professional-grade routing, short locates, and competitive per-share pricing.
description_long:
CenterPoint Securities is a direct market access (DMA) broker built specifically for active day traders who need professional-grade order routing and execution. The broker provides Level 2 data, direct market routing to specific exchanges and ECNs, and one of the most comprehensive short locate inventories in the retail brokerage space.

The short locate system is a standout feature — CenterPoint maintains relationships with multiple locate providers, giving traders access to hard-to-borrow shares that are unavailable at larger brokers. For short sellers, this alone can justify the higher per-share commissions. Order routing options include direct access to NYSE, NASDAQ, ARCA, BATS, and other venues, with the ability to route to specific market makers.

CenterPoint is a small operation (1-10 employees) focused entirely on serving active traders. This means personalized support but also limited technology development compared to larger brokers. The platform is DAS Trader-based, which is industry standard for DMA trading but not as polished as proprietary platforms from Schwab or Fidelity. Commissions start at $0.004/share with volume discounts available.
pros: One of the best short locate systems in retail brokerage for hard-to-borrow shares
Direct market access with routing to specific exchanges and ECNs
Personalized support from a small team focused exclusively on active traders
Per-share pricing with volume discounts suits high-frequency day traders
cons: Stocks only — no options, futures, or crypto
Per-share commissions are more expensive than commission-free brokers for low-volume traders
Very small team (1-10 people) limits technology development pace
DAS Trader platform has a steeper learning curve than modern broker apps
